my_thoughts: Use Polus?s contradiction as mirror: ask if they too will dodge truth to avoid shame. Expect resistance; if they defend Polus, press whether truth can be hidden without self-contradiction.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student defends Polus (?Yes. He was forced to contradict himself because he dared not say what he truly believed?)?defensive_rationalization via pushes_back. They understand: contradiction arose from fear, not logic. Recursion_loop/UM_06 will show that hiding belief creates self-contradiction again. Pattern: defense of Polus ? ready to mirror the trap.
visible_response: So you admit that shame compelled him—not understanding?
